title: Physical Integrity Rights Score
subtitle: The score captures the extent to which citizens are protected from government killings, torture, political imprisonments, extrajudicial executions, mass killings and disappearances. Large positive scores mean abuses are rare relative to other countries and years, large negative scores that abuses are relatively widespread.
caption:
Physical Integrity Rights Score, 2019

The score captures the extent to which citizens are protected from government killings, torture, political imprisonments, extrajudicial executions, mass killings and disappearances. Large positive scores mean abuses are rare relative to other countries and years, large negative scores that abuses are relatively widespread.

The map displays the Physical Integrity Rights Score for 2019 across the globe, using a color scale ranging from -4 to +4. This metric quantifies the protection of citizens from state-sponsored violence, where large positive scores indicate that abuses are rare and large negative scores indicate that abuses are relatively widespread. The visualization reveals a distinct geographic distribution of these scores. The highest values, represented by dark blue shades corresponding to scores between 3 and 4, are concentrated in western and northern Europe, including Norway, Sweden, and the United Kingdom, as well as in Canada, Australia, and New Zealand. In contrast, the lowest scores appear in the deep red band, indicating values between -4 and -3, which are visible in countries such as Algeria, Yemen, and Myanmar. A broad band of orange and light red colors, representing negative scores between -1 and -3, covers much of the Middle East, North Africa, and parts of Asia, including Russia, China, and Brazil. Meanwhile, the United States and Mexico fall into the lightest beige category, signifying scores near zero or slightly negative. Large areas of central Africa and Greenland are marked with diagonal hatching, denoting that no data is available for these regions. The data shows a clear clustering of high protection scores in the Global North and lower scores across the Global South and parts of Eastern Europe and Asia.
